title: Samanea schaffneri
taxonomy: Samanea schaffneri is classified within the family Fabaceae. This classification places it among the legume family, which includes a wide variety of plants. The genus Samanea is also part of the Fabaceae family, and it comprises several species of trees. The species Samanea schaffneri is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This classification provides insight into the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Samanea schaffneri within the broader context of the Fabaceae family.

intro: Samanea schaffneri, also known as the Puyan tree, is a species of flowering plant in the legume family Fabaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its purported health benefits such as antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Samanea schaffneri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma.
